lines: Jim is absolutely eating right now — Spain put four past Saudi Arabia (sorry Wilmot), and the Netherlands only went and tonked Sweden 5-1, meaning Jim's own Group F asset is already limping. Collecting points with one hand, destroying himself with the other. Classic Jim.

Wilmot watched Spain dismantle his Saudi Arabia side 4-0 and still has the audacity to be second in the standings. The optimism required to back Saudi Arabia in a World Cup sweepstake is a personality disorder.

Tunisia are cooked — two games, no points, four goals conceded, and both Callum and Dene are just watching their Group F investment turn to ash in real time. Dene in particular is one more bad result away from being mathematically irrelevant before the weekend.

The Foreman's Curaçao held Ecuador to a 0-0, which is either a heroic point or a metaphor for a man clinging to driftwood in the mid-Atlantic — either way, Türkiye are bottom of Group D with zero points, so the driftwood is shrinking fast.

Today's ones to watch: Ellis needs New Zealand to nick something against Egypt in Group G, and Croft desperately wants Uruguay to stop fannying about and beat Cabo Verde — a win puts Uruguay top of Group H and gives Croft something to actually smile about for once.
